Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-25 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum Ranker Long Today's data reveals a striking divergence in long-term momentum patterns between the top two ranked tickers. CVE (rank 1, score 92.2) shows exceptional recent performance with 126.62% one-year returns and 262.48% five-year returns, but a dramatic collapse in decade-long performance at 104.01% (nearly 60% below its five-year annualized rate), suggesting this energy stock has underperformed severely over the past five years despite recent strength. In sharp contrast, EACO (rank 2) demonstrates explosive long-term consistency with 1758.23% ten-year returns that dwarf its five-year return of 462.95%, indicating sustained outperformance across all time horizons and a 1494.08% advantage over SPY over the past decade. CVE's tight correlation with XLE (0.738) confirms its energy sector exposure, while its deteriorating ratio_10y_5y score of 0.4 signals meaningful deceleration, whereas EACO's ratio_10y_5y of 3.8 shows accelerating returns further back in time. Both stocks pass all SMA tests and have clean momentum signals, but the underlying compositions appear fundamentally different in their return sustainability and long-term trajectory.
